description: Two or more claims are 'letharo' when they can be true together under the same interpretation and conditions. They need not resemble one another, and consistency supplies no evidence that any claim is actually true. It only tells us that the claims can share a room without logical trouble.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and release into middle-height front 'e'. Set the tongue tip at the upper teeth for stressed unvoiced 'th', then open onto central 'a'. Finish with a brief alveolar tap or short trill before middle-height rounded 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The word moves from a lateral opening through broad dental breath and ends in a rounded tap. Its three gestures remain distinct but meet in one unbroken contour, a modest physical echo of claims that can stand together without becoming alike.
usage_notes: The adjective appears before plural claims or predicatively; the quality-noun rule supplies consistency. 'senao' (same) concerns identity or likeness, 'lei' (harmony) concerns a valued relation, and 'shewo' (true) assesses a claim against what is so.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Claims can coexist under one set of conditions and conflict under another; consistency remains scoped rather than absolute.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The term distinguishes genuine incompatibility from mere difference. That precision reduces pressure for unnecessary uniformity.

semantic_domains.cognition: the quality of propositions that can be true together
semantic_domains.wisdom: holding plurality without confusing compatibility with truth
